design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels using a yo-yo weapon to defeat enemies and collect magic points, defeat minigame bosses, progress through New York City stages toward confronting Mr. Green
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time limit per level balanced against enemy patterns and resource collection (magic points via scrolls)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as The Noid, Domino's mascot, using creative weapons (yo-yo, skateboard, autogyro) to save New York City and earn pizza rewards
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy marketing IP integration (advergame) with zero mechanical changes from original Japanese release; game prioritizes brand personality over mechanical innovation. Uses magic point resource system and screen-clearing special attacks as primary offensive strategy rather than direct combat

difficulty.lives_system: Single hit death; no life meter. Extra lives awarded per 20,000 points scored
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level checkpoint; must complete entire stage before time limit expires
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Resource management through magic point collection determines access to screen-clearing special attacks; no difficulty select documented

progression.upgrade_system: Magic points collected from scrolls enable screen-clearing special attacks and rare power-ups; points also trigger extra life awards at 20,000 increments
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage progression through New York City map; minigames and vehicle stages (skateboard, autogyro) are level-specific variations
replayability_hooks: Point accumulation system (extra life milestones at 20,000 points)
Varied level mechanics (skateboard, autogyro, minigame levels like Pizza Crusher)

world_building_techniques: Opening news bulletin exposition (Mr. Green threat, mayor's pizza reward)
NYC map screen setting and environmental theming (pier stage replacement, urban locations)
Domino's pizza marketing integration as plot reward
Thematic level variety (building stages, urban obstacles)

boss_design.philosophy: Localization-specific boss encounters: Japanese version uses card-based system (Hanamaru), Western version uses pizza-eating contests (Yo! Noid). Boss battles framed as thematic challenges rather than pattern-heavy combat
notable_bosses: Minigame-style boss encounters (pizza-eating contests in Yo! Noid)

faction_indicators: The Noid vs. Mr. Green (protagonist vs. doppelgänger antagonist)
Wild creatures (antagonistic forces under Mr. Green's control)
danger_signals: Mass hysteria in New York City caused by Mr. Green's creature army
Time limit mechanic signaling urgent threat
Single-hit death system emphasizing environmental hazard severity
environmental_storytelling: NYC pier setting (tropical island → urban conversion in localization)
Thematic level variety (building climbing stage with distinct sprite iteration)
Vehicle stages (skateboard, autogyro) showing environmental traversal options
Pizza-eating contest minigame reinforcing Domino's brand narrative

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Yo! Noid article)
